description: In MaglevGraphBuilder::TryBuildAndAllocateJSGeneratorObject(), a VirtualObject is created via CreateJSGeneratorObject() with slot_count derived from the generator function's initial_map instance_size. The zone allocator (zone()->AllocateArray<ValueNode*>(slot_count)) does not initialize the allocated slots. CreateJSGeneratorObject() only initializes the fixed JSGeneratorObject header fields (~10 or 12 slots), and TryBuildAndAllocateJSGeneratorObject() then initializes only slack_tracking_prediction.inobject_property_count() additional in-object property slots. With a corrupted initial_map (achievable via a prior sandbox bypass), an attacker can forge instance_size to be larger than the total of initialized fixed fields plus inobject_property_count, causing the trailing VirtualObject slots to remain uninitialized (containing zone-allocator garbage). These uninitialized ValueNode* slots are subsequently processed unconditionally by BuildInlinedAllocation() via VirtualObject::ForEachInput(), which passes each slot to GetTaggedValue(). GetTaggedValue() calls GetOrCreateInfoFor() which calls StaticTypeForNode(). StaticTypeForNode() contains two switch-cases: the first (over ValueRepresentation) is exhaustive but small enough that the compiler emits a comparison-based fallthrough for the tagged case, allowing an invalid representation value of 7 to bypass all early returns; the second switch (exhaustive over Opcode) is large enough that the compiler emits an unchecked jump-offset table lookup. By heap-spraying the zone memory with a crafted fake ValueNode whose opcode field contains an attacker-chosen out-of-bounds index, the unchecked jump table lookup redirects execution to an arbitrary code location, granting full program counter control and a complete V8 sandbox bypass.

fixes.description: Local patch generated from V8 commit 73f21fd09b6b2b9930dbed725bbbde78de4e1710 to make validation and patched-image builds independent of remote Gerrit fetches.
fixes.evidence_text: [maglev] Clear remaining slots for objects with slack
